prior.title: ICAP Allowance Price Explorer: Terms of Use and Data Documentation
prior.summary: Documentation for ICAP's Allowance Price Explorer tool, a platform providing ETS allowance pricing data across multiple jurisdictions. The resource outlines terms of use, data limitations, methodology notes, and source attribution requirements. Key notes include manual quarterly updates, non-comparable pricing across different ETS systems, use of different price types (auction vs. secondary market), and currency conversion methodology using IMF exchange rates.
prior.full_brief:
# ICAP Allowance Price Explorer — Terms of Use and Data Documentation

**Technology Profile | MONITORING**

---

## 1. What's Being Tested or Deployed and By Whom

The International Carbon Action Partnership (ICAP) operates the **Allowance Price Explorer (APE)**, a live interactive tool that tracks, visualises, and enables download of allowance prices and auction revenues from emissions trading systems worldwide.

**Enhanced version launched July 2025.** FACT: "The International Carbon Action Partnership (ICAP) is pleased to announce the launch of an enhanced version of its Allowance Price Explorer (APE) tool." *Source: ICAP Launches Enhanced Allowance Price Explorer Tool, ICAP, July 1 2025. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/news/icap-launches-enhanced-allowance-price-explorer-tool

**Data partners named at launch.** FACT: "ICAP acknowledges the data that has been generously facilitated by key partners, including SinoCarbon Innovation & Investment, Intercontinental Exchange, European Energy Exchange, and Carbon News, which have made these enhancements possible." *Source: ICAP Launches Enhanced Allowance Price Explorer Tool, ICAP, July 1 2025. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/news/icap-launches-enhanced-allowance-price-explorer-tool

**Expanded secondary-market price coverage added.** FACT: "users can now access expanded information on allowance prices in secondary markets for the California Cap-and-Trade program, the Regional Greenhouse Gas Initiative (RGGI), the United Kingdom Emissions Trading Scheme, and the Washington Cap-and-Invest program." *Source: ICAP Launches Enhanced Allowance Price Explorer Tool, ICAP, July 1 2025. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/news/icap-launches-enhanced-allowance-price-explorer-tool

**Revenue data now comprehensive across major systems.** FACT: "The renewed APE tool now features comprehensive revenue data from allowance sales across most major ETSs globally." *Source: ICAP Launches Enhanced Allowance Price Explorer Tool, ICAP, July 1 2025. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/news/icap-launches-enhanced-allowance-price-explorer-tool

**World Bank dependency confirmed.** FACT: "The International Carbon Action Partnership (ICAP) supports the dashboard with the latest data on Emissions Trading Systems (ETS)." *Source: State and Trends of Carbon Pricing Dashboard — About, World Bank, accessed 2026. https://carbonpricingdashboard.worldbank.org/about

**ETS landscape context.** FACT: "41 emissions trading systems (ETS) are now in force worldwide, covering 26% of global GHG emissions." *Source: Emissions Trading Worldwide: ICAP Status Report 2026, ICAP, April 14 2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/publications/emissions-trading-worldwide-icap-status-report-2026

FACT: "ETS revenues reached a new record of nearly USD 80 billion in 2025." *Source: Emissions Trading Worldwide: ICAP Status Report 2026, ICAP, April 14 2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/publications/emissions-trading-worldwide-icap-status-report-2026

**Systems currently excluded from the tool.** FACT: "Prices for the ETS systems in Kazakhstan, Tokyo, Saitama, Oregon, Austria, Australia and Indonesia are not included in this version of the tool." *Source: Terms of Use and Documentation Notes — Allowance Price Explorer, ICAP. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/terms-use-and-documentation-notes-allowance-price-explorer

**Data update cadence — critical operational limitation.** FACT: "Allowance prices and revenues are updated manually and quarterly." *Source: Terms of Use and Documentation Notes — Allowance Price Explorer, ICAP. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/terms-use-and-documentation-notes-allowance-price-explorer

*Operational implication:* For workspaces using the APE as a carbon cost benchmarking input in client reporting or freight tender submissions, the quarterly manual update cycle means spot prices displayed can lag actual market levels by up to three months. This is not a defect in the tool — it is the documented design — but it imposes a ceiling on how current any APE-derived figure can be claimed to be in commercial or compliance documents.

---

## 2. What This Tells Us About Industry Trajectory

**Carbon pricing is expanding rapidly and is now central to global trade.** FACT: "Three new national-level systems launching in 2026 — Japan, India, and Vietnam — with more in the pipeline, reflecting the spread of emissions trading across diverse economies and development contexts." *Source: Emissions Trading Worldwide: ICAP Status Report 2026, ICAP, April 14 2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/publications/emissions-trading-worldwide-icap-status-report-2026

FACT: "An ETS is now in place in 14 of the G20 nations — and several have named it as a central instrument for NDC 3.0 delivery under the Paris Agreement." *Source: Emissions Trading Worldwide: ICAP Status Report 2026, ICAP, April 14 2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/publications/emissions-trading-worldwide-icap-status-report-2026

FACT: "Jurisdictions operating an ETS together account for 63% of global GDP and more than half the world's population — placing carbon markets at the centre of the global economy." *Source: Emissions Trading Worldwide: ICAP Status Report 2026, ICAP, April 14 2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/publications/emissions-trading-worldwide-icap-status-report-2026

*Analytical inference:* The trajectory for freight forwarders is clear: the proportion of trade lanes passing through at least one ETS-regulated jurisdiction is expanding every year. The APE tool's coverage of 41 active systems across all major trade corridors makes it the leading public reference point for Scope 3 carbon cost signals embedded in ocean, air, and road freight. Workspaces that learn to use it accurately gain a referencing advantage in client sustainability conversations.

**CBAM compliance phase underway and linked directly to ETS pricing.** FACT: "the EU's CBAM has entered its compliance phase — and the UK is following suit — acting as a catalyst for broader carbon pricing ambitions in trading partner countries." *Source: Emissions Trading Worldwide: ICAP Status Report 2026, ICAP, April 14 2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/publications/emissions-trading-worldwide-icap-status-report-2026

*Analytical inference:* For workspaces handling imports of CBAM-covered goods into the EU or UK, EU ETS allowance prices tracked in the APE are directly relevant to the embedded carbon cost calculations that importers will need to report and eventually pay. The APE's EU ETS price series is therefore not merely background intelligence — it is an input upstream of an active compliance obligation.

**China ETS moving toward absolute cap — price signal becoming structurally more important.** FACT: "China has issued landmark guidelines to transition its national ETS to an absolute cap by 2027 and progressively expand coverage to all major industrial emitters." *Source: Emissions Trading Worldwide: ICAP Status Report 2026, ICAP, April 14 2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/publications/emissions-trading-worldwide-icap-status-report-2026

*Analytical inference:* For workspaces operating on Asia–Europe and transpacific trade lanes, the China ETS price — currently tracked in the APE via Shanghai Environmental and Energy Exchange data — will become a progressively larger cost factor for Chinese counterparts and an increasingly material Scope 3 signal for freight carbon accounting.

---

## 3. Supplier Access and Procurement Reality

The APE is a free public tool with access available at https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets-prices. No licensing fee or registration is documented in the source materials.

**Non-commercial use restriction applies to all users.** FACT: "Graphics or underlying data provided by the ICAP Allowance Price Explorer may only be reproduced, redistributed and used for non-commercial purposes subject to ICAP's prior written permission." *Source: Terms of Use and Documentation Notes — Allowance Price Explorer, ICAP. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/terms-use-and-documentation-notes-allowance-price-explorer

*Legal Confirmation Required:* Whether using APE data or graphics in a client sustainability report, a carbon surcharge calculation distributed to clients, or a freight tender response constitutes "commercial use" requiring ICAP's prior written permission is a legal question that the source documents do not resolve. Workspaces intending to reproduce or redistribute APE data beyond internal use should seek legal confirmation and, if necessary, written permission from ICAP at info@icapcarbonaction.com before publication.

**Attribution obligation on all permitted uses.** FACT: "When permitted to use graphics or underlying data provided by the ICAP Allowance Price Explorer, users are required to reference both the ICAP Allowance Price Explorer and the original source of the data (as described below), as well as any disclaimers identified in the sources." *Source: Terms of Use and Documentation Notes — Allowance Price Explorer, ICAP. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/terms-use-and-documentation-notes-allowance-price-explorer

**ICE data carries additional commercial prohibition.** FACT: "ANY DISTRIBUTION OR COMMERCIAL USE OF ICE FUTURES DATA IS PROHIBITED WITHOUT THE PRIOR WRITTEN CONSENT OF ICE DATA LLP." *Source: Terms of Use and Documentation Notes — Allowance Price Explorer, ICAP. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/terms-use-and-documentation-notes-allowance-price-explorer

*Legal Confirmation Required:* The ICE disclaimer quoted above applies specifically to ICE Futures data used within the APE, which includes EU ETS (from January 2019), California Cap-and-Trade (from January 2019), UK ETS (from May 2021), and Washington Cap-and-Invest (from June 2023) price series. Whether any forwarding or inclusion of these price series in client-facing materials constitutes "distribution or commercial use" under ICE's terms requires legal confirmation before any external use.

**Download lag for ICE weekly average data.** FACT: "Weekly average ICE Data is available for download and with a 6-month lag." *Source: Terms of Use and Documentation Notes — Allowance Price Explorer, ICAP. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/terms-use-and-documentation-notes-allowance-price-explorer

*Operational implication:* For workspaces needing current ICE-sourced price data (EU ETS, UK ETS, California, Washington) for near-real-time carbon cost calculations, the downloadable data carries a six-month lag by design. End-of-day ICE data is visible in the graph view only and cannot be downloaded. This constrains use in time-sensitive commercial applications unless sourced directly from ICE or another licensed provider.

**Currency conversion methodology — IMF exchange rates, with known timing gap.** FACT: "For currency conversions, monthly exchange rates provided by the International Monetary Fund (IMF) are used. It is possible that, at the time of updating the tool, exchange rates for the respective month are not yet available from IMF. In this instance, the latest available exchange rates are used." *Source: Terms of Use and Documentation Notes — Allowance Price Explorer, ICAP. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/terms-use-and-documentation-notes-allowance-price-explorer

**RGGI unit conversion — short tons to metric tons.** FACT: "RGGI allowance prices are in short tons and have been converted metric tons for the purposes of the tool." *Source: Terms of Use and Documentation Notes — Allowance Price Explorer, ICAP. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/terms-use-and-documentation-notes-allowance-price-explorer

**Prices not inflation-adjusted.** FACT: "Allowance prices are reported in nominal values and are not inflation adjusted." *Source: Terms of Use and Documentation Notes — Allowance Price Explorer, ICAP. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/terms-use-and-documentation-notes-allowance-price-explorer

*Analytical inference:* For multi-year trend comparisons used in client sustainability narratives or internal carbon budget modelling, the absence of inflation adjustment means that nominal price series may overstate real price growth in periods of high inflation. Workspaces citing historical APE price data over multi-year horizons should note this limitation explicitly.

---

## 4. Operational Fit by Transport Mode and Cargo Vertical

**Air (primary mode):**
*Operational implication:* Air freight on routes through the EU (including flights on EU ETS scope — intra-EEA and, as of the current compliance phase, in connection with CORSIA) generates Scope 1 emissions for the carrier and Scope 3 emissions for the workspace as shipper. EU ETS allowance prices tracked in the APE (ICE data, end-of-day visible in graph, weekly average downloadable with six-month lag) represent the carbon cost signal relevant to air carrier surcharge pass-through modelling. For premium verticals — live events, fine art, luxury goods, film and TV production, high-value automotive — where air is the dominant mode, the EU ETS price series is the most operationally relevant line in the APE.

FACT: "Different types of prices are displayed for different systems, either auction or from the secondary market." *Source: Terms of Use and Documentation Notes — Allowance Price Explorer, ICAP. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/terms-use-and-documentation-notes-allowance-price-explorer

*Analytical inference:* Air carriers purchasing EU ETS allowances at auction or on the secondary market will pass costs through via fuel surcharges or explicit carbon surcharges. The APE's EU ETS price series provides a public reference for benchmarking whether carrier-quoted surcharges are proportionate to prevailing allowance prices, but the manual quarterly update cadence and six-month download lag limit its use for real-time verification.

**Road (secondary mode):**
*Operational implication:* Road freight within the EU will be subject to EU ETS 2, which covers buildings and road transport. FACT: "the EU is preparing to launch ETS 2 — covering buildings and road transport — in 2028." *Source: Emissions Trading Worldwide: ICAP Status Report 2026, ICAP, April 14 2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/publications/emissions-trading-worldwide-icap-status-report-2026

*Analytical inference:* ETS 2 pricing is not yet covered in the APE — it does not exist as a traded market — but when it launches in 2028 it will represent a new carbon cost layer on EU road freight. Workspaces moving goods by road within Europe should monitor whether ICAP adds ETS 2 to the APE's coverage as the market develops. This is a forward-intelligence gap to re-check in 2027.

**Ocean (tertiary mode):**
*Operational implication:* Ocean freight on EU-connected routes entered EU ETS scope for shipping in 2024. EU ETS allowance prices in the APE are therefore directly relevant to ocean carrier surcharge calculations for EU port calls. The APE does not currently include a dedicated shipping-specific ETS data series, but EU ETS prices apply to vessels above 5,000 GT calling at EU ports. Effect on ocean verticals requires carrier-specific data for exact surcharge calculation.

---

## 5. Competitive Positioning Implications for the Workspace

*Analytical inference:* Workspaces that actively use and accurately cite the APE in client conversations are differentiated from competitors who either avoid carbon pricing data (limiting their credibility) or cite it without understanding the tool's limitations (creating legal and reputational exposure). The competitive edge is not in having access — the tool is public — but in knowing what it can and cannot support.

*Analytical inference:* The non-commercial use restriction and ICE data prohibition create a compliance trap for competitors that reproduce APE graphics in commercial pitch materials without seeking ICAP permission. Workspaces that understand this constraint and either obtain permission or substitute permissible language are protected; those that do not are exposed. This is not a competitive advantage to exploit against competitors — it is a compliance risk to manage internally.

**No named competitor activity sourced** in the provided source blocks. Competitor-specific positioning in use of the APE cannot be reported without sourced evidence. This sub-section is therefore limited to the structural observation above.

---

## 6. Conversational and Strategic Talking Points

**What the workspace can credibly say to clients:**

- "ICAP's Allowance Price Explorer is the leading public reference for ETS allowance prices across 41 active systems worldwide, updated quarterly, and covers the EU ETS, UK ETS, California, RGGI, Korea, China, and other major systems."
- "Prices in the tool reflect either auction settlement prices or secondary market prices, depending on the system — they are not all the same type of data, and they are not directly comparable across systems."
- "EU ETS prices are sourced from ICE and EEX and represent the most liquid carbon market globally. They are the primary signal for estimating carbon cost pass-through on EU air and ocean freight."
- "Three new national ETS systems are launching in 2026 — Japan, India, and Vietnam — meaning the share of your supply chain covered by a carbon price is growing."
- "ETS revenues globally reached a record USD 80 billion in 2025. Carbon pricing is no longer marginal — it is embedded in the cost structure of freight across the major trade lanes."

**What to avoid saying:**

- Do not represent APE data as real-time. FACT: "Allowance price and revenue data updated manually." *Source: Terms of Use and Documentation Notes — Allowance Price Explorer, ICAP. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/terms-use-and-documentation-notes-allowance-price-explorer The tool updates quarterly; current prices may differ.
- Do not reproduce APE charts or tables in client-facing commercial materials without confirming whether ICAP's prior written permission is required and obtained, and whether the specific price series is subject to the ICE commercial use prohibition.
- Do not present prices from different ETS systems as directly comparable. FACT: "Allowance prices are not directly comparable across systems, given different circumstances and differences in ETS design." *Source: Terms of Use and Documentation Notes — Allowance Price Explorer, ICAP. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/terms-use-and-documentation-notes-allowance-price-explorer
- Do not present APE data as inflation-adjusted. The tool reports nominal values only.
- Do not cite APE price data for Kazakhstan, Tokyo, Saitama, Oregon, Austria, Australia, or Indonesia — these systems are explicitly excluded from the current version.

**Questions to pose to clients:**
- "Which of your shipments move through jurisdictions where an ETS is already in force? We can map that against the APE coverage list."
- "Are you receiving carbon surcharge line items from your carriers? Would it help to benchmark those against the prevailing allowance price for the relevant ETS?"
- "As CBAM's compliance phase is now active for EU imports, do you have visibility on the embedded carbon cost of the goods you're importing?"

---

## 7. Time-to-Market, Procurement Window, and Action

**The tool is live now.** The enhanced APE launched July 1 2025 and is accessible at https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets-prices. No procurement action, subscription fee, or access request is required.

**Immediate actions for the workspace:**

1. **Internal tool familiarisation — now.** Assign a sustainability or operations team member to complete the ICAP step-by-step user guide (https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/introducing-allowance-price-explorer) before using APE data in any client-facing context.

2. **Legal review of use terms — before any external publication.** *Legal Confirmation Required:* Confirm with legal counsel whether any current or planned use of APE data (in client reports, tender responses, sustainability communications, carbon surcharge disclosures) constitutes "commercial use" or "distribution" triggering ICAP's prior written permission requirement and/or the ICE commercial use prohibition. Contact ICAP at info@icapcarbonaction.com if permission is required.

3. **Attribution protocol — before any external publication.** Establish an internal citation standard that references both the ICAP Allowance Price Explorer and the original data source (EEX, ICE, RGGI, ARB, etc.) and carries the ICAP disclaimer language, as required by the terms of use.

4. **Quarterly monitoring cadence — set now.** Align internal carbon benchmarking reviews to the APE's quarterly update cycle. Flag to client-facing teams that inter-quarter APE data should be labelled as of the last quarterly update date, not as current.

5. **ETS 2 forward-watch — re-check Q4 2027.** *Analytical inference:* EU ETS 2, covering road transport from 2028, is not yet in the APE. Workspaces with material EU road freight exposure should re-check ICAP coverage in late 2027 to assess whether ETS 2 pricing has been added, and what that means for road freight carbon cost modelling.

6. **New ETS coverage watch — re-check Q1 2027.** Japan, India, and Vietnam national ETS systems are launching in 2026. *Analytical inference:* ICAP will likely add these systems to the APE as price data becomes available, given ICAP's stated role as a knowledge repository for global ETS. Workspaces with significant Asia-Pacific trade lane exposure should monitor the APE coverage list at the next annual cycle.

**MONITORING — re-check window: Q1 2027** for Japan/India/Vietnam ETS price data inclusion in APE; **Q4 2027** for EU ETS 2 coverage addition.

---

## New Sources Identified

| Source Name | URL | Tier estimate (1–7) | Why this source matters |
|---|---|---|---|
| ICAP Allowance Price Explorer — Live Tool | https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets-prices | 2 | Primary interactive implementation of the APE; confirms live price and revenue tracking scope and directly demonstrates the tool's current system coverage and currency. |
| ICAP APE Step-by-Step User Guide | https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/introducing-allowance-price-explorer | 2 | ICAP's own navigation guide for the APE; provides operational detail on features and cross-links to the terms documentation; essential for workspace teams adopting the tool. |
| ICAP Launches Enhanced Allowance Price Explorer Tool (July 2025) | https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/news/icap-launches-enhanced-allowance-price-explorer-tool | 2 | Official ICAP announcement naming data partners (ICE, EEX, SinoCarbon, Carbon News) and new secondary-market and revenue data features; provides sourced, dated confirmation of the July 2025 enhancement. |
| Emissions Trading Worldwide: ICAP Status Report 2026 | https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/publications/emissions-trading-worldwide-icap-status-report-2026 | 1 | Most current (April 2026) authoritative ICAP publication; provides verified ETS coverage count, revenue record, and policy trajectory that underlies the APE dataset and contextualises its operational importance. |
| World Bank Carbon Pricing Dashboard — About & Methodology | https://carbonpricingdashboard.worldbank.org/about | 2 | Confirms ICAP as the official ETS data supplier to the World Bank dashboard; establishes ICAP's institutional standing and the downstream use of APE-sourced data in policy-level instruments. |
| OECD Effective Carbon Rates 2025 — ETS Data Sources & Methodology | https://www.oecd.org/en/publications/effective-carbon-rates-2025_a5a5d71f-en/full-report/data-sources-and-methodology-for-emissions-trading-systems-modelling_39caf59d.html | 2 | Authoritative OECD annex on how ETS permit prices (auction vs secondary) are sourced and averaged; contextualises the APE's heterogeneous price-type disclosure and validates the methodological limitations the terms of use document. |

---
